title: Guarantee globally unique comment IDs
description:
As a developer consuming events, I want every comment to have a globally unique ID, so that comment_added events can reference comments unambiguously across storage and APIs.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Audit current comment creation/storage to confirm uniqueness and persistence of comment_id.
- If any gap exists, add UUID generation, persistence, and migration for existing comments.
- Event writer relies solely on comment_id (no fallbacks to text or position).
creator: ryan

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Verified comment IDs are globally unique in Rust/Python for project issues; added deterministic UUID assignment for Beads comments in Python migration and event logging uses canonical UUIDs.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Completed: comment IDs are UUIDs in standard issues; beads migration assigns deterministic uuid5 IDs and event writers use canonical comment IDs (no comment text stored).
